brands.name: Firehouse Subs
brands.notes: Firefighter-founded sub sandwich chain (Jacksonville, FL), owned by Restaurant Brands International since 2021. Strong Item 19 disclosure and aggressive 2026-2028 development incentives (up to $100K-$150K cash per restaurant) targeting all 50 states.

riskFlags.title: Multi-unit franchisee Chapter 11 bankruptcy (2026)
riskFlags.detail: CN Holdings LLC, an 11-unit Firehouse Subs franchisee in Utah and Idaho, filed Chapter 11 in March 2026, citing cost overruns and delays on new restaurants required under a development agreement signed in late 2023.

riskFlags.title: Prior franchisee Chapter 11 bankruptcy (2023)
riskFlags.detail: A Minnesota Firehouse Subs franchisee filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in 2023, part of a pattern of individual operator distress despite system-level growth.

riskFlags.title: Combined ongoing fees of 11% are above sandwich-segment norms
riskFlags.detail: Franchisees pay a 6% royalty plus a 5% national/local marketing contribution (11% of gross sales total), at the high end for the sandwich QSR segment; FDD-derived mean EBITDA margin is about 9% of sales.
riskFlags.source: Firehouse Subs Franchising (official) / Franchise Chatter FDD review

nationwide.note: "We have franchise growth opportunities across all 50 U.S. states and select Canadian provinces and territories." Priority development markets include NYC, LA, Chicago, Boston, Philadelphia, Seattle, Washington DC and Minneapolis.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Franchisor also requires $300,000 minimum net worth.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Low end for in-line traditional restaurant ($379,650-$795,600); end cap w/ drive-thru $549,650-$1,038,100; free-standing w/ drive-thru up to $1,396,100.

totalInvestmentMax.note: High end is free-standing restaurant with drive-thru.

adFundPercent.note: 5% contribution to national and local marketing funds.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19 (2025 FDD): mean 2024 annual sales of 1,022 traditional development restaurants; median $926,456. Free-standing drive-thru units averaged $1,085,557.

totalUnits.note: US FDD Item 20 count at end of 2024: 1,206 franchised + 42 company-owned. Parent RBI reported 1,449 systemwide (global) at end of 2025 with net restaurant growth of 7.7%.

yearFranchising.note: Entrepreneur lists 1995; Franchise Chatter's FDD review says franchising relaunched in 2001 after an early setback.

trainingWeeks.note: Franchisor states 6-7 weeks of initial training; lower bound shown.

employeesRequired.note: Entrepreneur lists 15-20 employees; lower bound shown.

item19Available.note: Item 19 discloses sales by format plus EBITDA for 665 reporting units.

veteranDiscount.note: Development incentive program for veterans and first responders; Entrepreneur lists up to $100,000 cash contribution per restaurant for first three restaurants.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Development Agreements with incentives for committing to multiple locations; many franchisees are multi-unit owners.

exclusiveTerritories.note: Entrepreneur lists exclusive territories as available; franchisor describes a defined territory granted under the Development Agreement.

isGrowing.note: Per RBI's 10-K, 2025 systemwide sales up 8.6% to $1.3B and net restaurant growth up 7.7% to 1,449 locations; FDD shows +36 net franchised units in 2024.

marketingSupport.note: Funded by the 5% national and local marketing contribution.

brands.name: Arby's
brands.notes: Roast-beef-led sandwich QSR founded 1964, owned by Inspire Brands; second-largest sandwich chain in the world with ~3,400+ restaurants. Mature system with strong Item 19 AUV (~$1.27M) but flat-to-declining US unit count and limited availability for brand-new franchisees (growth driven largely by existing multi-unit operators). Franchise development site: arbysfranchising.com.

riskFlags.title: 25-unit franchisee Chapter 11 bankruptcy (second filing)
riskFlags.detail: Miracle Restaurant Group, a 25-unit Arby's franchisee, filed Chapter 11 in June 2024 citing weak sales and high costs; it previously filed for bankruptcy in 2010 when it owned 60 Arby's locations.

riskFlags.title: US unit count contracting; quiet closures across multiple states
riskFlags.detail: Arby's ended 2024 with 3,365 US locations, a 48-site decrease vs 2023, and closed at least 14 more locations across eight states in early 2025 (including four in the Jacksonville, FL area).

riskFlags.title: 70 units ceased operations per latest FDD Item 20
riskFlags.detail: The 2026 FDD Item 20 system data shows 70 ceased operations, alongside low attrition signals (8 non-renewals, 57 transfers) and 115 reacquired units sold to franchisees.

riskFlags.title: New-franchisee availability currently limited
riskFlags.detail: Third-party franchise portals report Arby's is not currently accepting new applicants, noting the brand 'has reached its current goals thanks to high demand, with more opportunities coming soon'; no open-market list is published by the franchisor.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Entrepreneur (2026): $644,950-$2,451,000. FranDB's 2026 FDD analysis lists $651,550-$2,456,600 — minor variance between FDD years/formats.

franchiseFee.note: $37,500 for the first unit, $25,000 for each subsequent unit; Entrepreneur shows the $12,500-$37,500 range.

royaltyPercent.note: 4% of gross sales for traditional restaurants; 6.2% for non-traditional restaurants.

adFundPercent.note: 1.2% of gross sales to the franchisee-owned marketing association/national advertising, plus a required minimum 3% local store marketing spend. Entrepreneur lists a combined 5.2% ad royalty; FranDB shows 3.22%-5.0%.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19 (2026 FDD): average gross revenue of 2,182 franchised traditional restaurants open the full year 2025; median $1,201,669.

totalUnits.note: Entrepreneur 2026 systemwide count (+0.3%). 2026 FDD Item 20 (FranDB) lists 2,344 franchised + 921 company-owned; US count was 3,365 at end of 2024 (down 48 vs 2023).

closureCount.note: Ceased operations per 2026 FDD Item 20 system health data; reporting period not specified by source.

yearFounded.note: First Arby's opened in Boardman, Ohio.

trainingWeeks.note: Derived from 336 hours of on-the-job training listed by Entrepreneur (approx. 8+ weeks full-time).

employeesRequired.note: Entrepreneur lists 15-25 employees; lower bound shown.

veteranDiscount.note: 50% discount on the development fee, license fee waived, and a royalty reduction in year one for honorably discharged veterans.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Reduced $25,000 license fee for each subsequent unit; system growth driven by large multi-unit operators (e.g., AES Restaurant Group at 344 stores).

absenteeOwnership.note: Entrepreneur lists absentee ownership as allowed.

brands.name: Little Caesars
brands.notes: Third-largest pizza chain in the world (behind Pizza Hut and Domino's), founded in Detroit in 1959 and family-owned (Ilitch). Carryout-focused model with one of the lowest entry costs among major pizza brands, but the FDD contains no Item 19 financial performance representation. Franchise development site: franchise.littlecaesars.com.

riskFlags.title: No Item 19 financial performance representation in FDD
riskFlags.detail: Little Caesars does not disclose unit-level financial performance (Item 19) in its FDD, making it harder for prospects to verify revenue and profit claims before buying.

riskFlags.title: Multi-unit franchisee in repeat Chapter 11 bankruptcy
riskFlags.detail: Red Door Pizza LLC, a multi-unit Little Caesars (and Taco John's) franchisee, filed Chapter 11 again in 2025 — it operated 31 restaurants at its December 2022 filing and 15 at its 2025 filing.

riskFlags.title: EEOC racial harassment and retaliation suit against franchisee
riskFlags.detail: The EEOC sued Target Market Enterprises, a Western Washington Little Caesars franchisee, in September 2025 for failing to stop racial harassment of Black employees at its Mount Vernon, WA store and for firing an employee who complained.

nationwide.note: Actively franchising across the US with regional franchise managers; the site describes 'accelerating franchise developments by targeting key markets across the country' and shows per-market availability tiers (Significant/Multiple/Limited Opportunities) rather than restricting to specific states.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Minimum $200,000 liquid assets and $400,000 net worth.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Franchisor site lists $376,500-$1,769,200; the 2025 FDD Item 7 range was $446,500-$1,817,200 (Entrepreneur/FDD Exchange).

totalInvestmentMax.note: 2025 FDD listed $1,817,200 as the high end; franchisor site currently shows $1,769,200.

royaltyPercent.note: Greater of 6% of gross sales or a weekly minimum (reported as $300/week).

adFundPercent.note: Franchisor site and Entrepreneur list 5% marketing; some FDD-derived sources cite a 3%-7% combined marketing/brand fund range.

totalUnits.note: Entrepreneur 2026 count (+2.3%); Franchise Investor Data breaks this into 3,705 franchised + 580 company-owned US locations.

trainingWeeks.note: Derived from 250 hours on-the-job + 64 hours classroom training (approx. 7-8 weeks full-time).

employeesRequired.note: Entrepreneur lists 10-50 staff; lower bound shown.

item19Available.note: No financial performance representation in the FDD.

veteranDiscount.note: Veterans Program: $5,000-$20,000 off franchise fee, equipment and initial food order discounts; enhanced package for disabled veterans and Gold Star families.

multiUnitAvailable.note: System includes multi-unit operators (e.g., 31-store franchisee); franchisor targets growth-minded operators in key markets.

isGrowing.note: +2.3% unit growth per Entrepreneur 2026; net addition of 69 units in 2024.

minFootprint.note: Non-traditional locations run 470-1,399 sq ft; traditional carryout stores are larger.

brands.name: Zaxbys
brands.notes: Athens, GA-based chicken fingers and wings QSR founded 1990; surpassed 1,000 restaurants across 22 states in early 2026 as it expands beyond its Southeast core (Las Vegas, Phoenix, Philadelphia, New Jersey, Maryland). Very strong Item 19 AUV (~$2.78M) but one of the highest investment requirements in the chicken segment. Franchise site: zaxbys.com/franchise.

riskFlags.title: High investment and capital requirements vs segment norms
riskFlags.detail: Total investment of $1,445,000-$3,810,500 with $750,000 liquid capital and $1,000,000 net worth required — among the highest entry bars in chicken QSR franchising.

riskFlags.title: Negligence lawsuit naming Zaxby's corporate entities over franchise-location assault
riskFlags.detail: A November 2025 suit in Pulaski County, AR (Case 60CV-25-13586) names Zaxby's Franchising LLC, Zaxby's Operating Company LP and franchisee DND Groups, alleging negligent hiring/supervision after a manager at a Sherwood, AR location was charged with sexually assaulting a minor employee.

statesOpen.note: Named a key growth market for 2026.
statesOpen.source: International Franchise Association / Zaxbys press release

statesOpen.note: Key growth market for 2026; also cited for 'immense availability' in 2025.
statesOpen.source: International Franchise Association / Zaxbys press release

statesOpen.note: Key growth market for 2026; Northwest Indiana development agreement signed April 2025.
statesOpen.source: International Franchise Association / Zaxbys press release

statesOpen.note: Named a key growth market for 2026.
statesOpen.source: International Franchise Association / Zaxbys press release

statesOpen.note: Key growth market for 2026; also cited for footprint availability in 2025.
statesOpen.source: International Franchise Association / Zaxbys press release

statesOpen.note: Named a key growth market for 2026.
statesOpen.source: International Franchise Association / Zaxbys press release

statesOpen.note: Named a key growth market for 2026.
statesOpen.source: International Franchise Association / Zaxbys press release

statesOpen.note: Named a key growth market for 2026.
statesOpen.source: International Franchise Association / Zaxbys press release

statesOpen.note: Named a key growth market for 2026.
statesOpen.source: International Franchise Association / Zaxbys press release

statesOpen.note: Cited as having 'immense availability' for footprint growth.
statesOpen.source: International Franchise Association / Zaxbys press release

statesOpen.note: Cited as having 'immense availability' for footprint growth.
statesOpen.source: International Franchise Association / Zaxbys press release

statesOpen.note: Cited as having 'immense availability'; 7-store Missouri development agreement signed February 2026.
statesOpen.source: International Franchise Association / Zaxbys press release

statesOpen.note: Cited as having 'immense availability' for footprint growth.
statesOpen.source: International Franchise Association / Zaxbys press release

adFundPercent.note: Marketing contribution up to 4% of weekly gross sales (Entrepreneur lists 3%-4%).

avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19: average 2024 gross revenue of 776 franchised restaurants open the full year; median $2,710,374; range $1.0M-$6.3M.

totalUnits.note: Surpassed 1,000 restaurants across 22 states as of January 2026; FDD Item 20 showed 826 franchised + 143 company-owned at end of 2024.

trainingWeeks.note: Derived from 135-400 hours on-the-job plus 32.5 hours classroom training (lower bound approx. 4 weeks full-time).

item19Available.note: Item 19 includes revenue plus quartile cost-of-goods/labor data for reporting franchised restaurants.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Multi-store development agreements actively signed (e.g., 7-store Missouri deal, February 2026).

isGrowing.note: +30 net franchised units in 2024; targeting 60+ openings in 2026 with a long-term goal of 100 new stores annually.

brands.name: Smoothie King
brands.notes: Original US smoothie franchise (founded 1973, Dallas-based, privately held) with 1,200+ units nationwide. US system is growing steadily (74 openings and 101 new franchise commitments in 2025) though the brand exited South Korea in 2025. Franchise development site: smoothiekingfranchise.com, which publishes the full FDD.

riskFlags.title: Exited South Korea market (2025)
riskFlags.detail: Smoothie King's Korean master franchise (Shinsegae Food) ceased operations by October 2025 after contract termination, closing the remaining ~90 stores (down from 305 in 2021). This drives the negative worldwide unit trend (-8% per Entrepreneur) despite US growth.

riskFlags.title: Litigation disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: October 2025 settlement of an employee stock-option lawsuit (options repurchased at $1,750 each), and an ongoing civil action by multiple plaintiffs against Smoothie King Franchises, Inc. & SK USA, Inc. in US District Court (E.D.), per FDD disclosures.

riskFlags.title: Same-store sales flat in 2024
riskFlags.detail: Average same-store sales growth was 0.00% in 2024 vs 2023 (after +11.64% in 2023 vs 2022), per the Item 19 data in the FDD; average unit net sales dipped slightly from $661,316 (2023) to $659,567 (2024).

nationwide.note: 2025 development spanned 47 unique DMAs nationwide with first-ever stores in Utah and Minnesota; a 2026 franchisee incentive program targets AR, AZ, CO, IL, MA, MI, MN, NM, OK, PA, UT, VA and WI. Franchisor site offers an available-territories map.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Inline/endcap locations $346,350-$679,465; freestanding drive-thru locations up to $1,277,650.

totalInvestmentMax.note: High end is freestanding location with drive-thru.

royaltyPercent.note: 6% of gross sales, subject to a $500 monthly minimum.

adFundPercent.note: 3% national marketing fee; rises to 5% if required 2% local marketing spend is not met where no regional fund exists.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19: average net sales of 1,026 franchised units for the 13-month period ending Dec 30, 2024. Franchisor site cites $828,761 average gross sales for the TOP 50% of traditional franchised units for calendar year ending Dec 29, 2025.

totalUnits.note: US FDD Item 20 at end of 2024: 1,149 franchised + 52 company-owned. Franchisor cites 1,200+ units nationwide; Entrepreneur lists 1,265 worldwide (-8%, reflecting the South Korea exit).

trainingWeeks.note: Derived from 100-240 hours on-the-job plus 74-102 hours classroom training (lower bound approx. 4 weeks full-time).

employeesRequired.note: Entrepreneur lists 10-15 staff; lower bound shown.

fddAvailable.note: Full 2026-27 FDD published on the franchisor's own website.

item19Available.note: Franchisor publicly cites Item 19-based average gross sales figures.

veteranDiscount.note: 20% off franchise fee; ranked #11 in Entrepreneur's 2025 Top Franchises for Veterans.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Actively signs multi-unit development deals; ranked #11 in Entrepreneur's 2025 Top Brands for Multi-Unit Owners.

isGrowing.note: 74 new US stores opened and 101 franchise commitments signed in 2025; 90+ openings forecast for 2026. US net franchised units +56 in 2024 (worldwide count fell due to South Korea exit).
